perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, Merchant operates retail stores and desires that the Agent act as Merchant's exclusive agent for the purposes of: (a) selling all of the Merchandise (as hereinafter defined) from Merchant's retail store locations identified on Exhibit 1(a)(1) attached hereto (each a "Store" and collectively the "Stores") and distribution centers (including e-commerce 1 Merchant consists of The Bon-Ton Stores, Inc.; The Bon-Ton Department Stores, Inc. ("BTDS"); The Bon-Ton Giftco, LLC; Carson Pirie Scott II, Inc.; Bon- Ton Distribution, LLC; McRIL, LLC; Bonstores Holdings One, LLC; Bonstores Realty One, LLC; Bonstores Holdings Two, LLC; and Bonstores Realty Two, LLC. Case 18-10248- MFW Doc 632-1 Filed 04/18/18 Page 3 of 60 facilities) identified on Exhibit 1(a)(2) attached hereto (each a "Distribution Center" and collectively, the "Distribution Centers") by means of a "going out of business," "store closing," "sale on everything," "everything must go," or similar sale as described further below (the "GOB Sale"), with the nature and manner of advertising the GOB Sale being in Agent's sole discretion, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the Sale Guidelines and Approval Order (each as defined below);
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, Merchant operates retail stores and desires that the Agent act as Merchant's non-exclusive agent for the purposes of: (a) selling all of the Merchandise (as hereinafter defined) from Merchant's retail store locations identified on Exhibit 1(a)(1) attached hereto (each a "Store" and collectively the "Stores") and distribution centers (including e-commerce 1 Merchant consists of The Bon-Ton Stores, Inc.; The Bon-Ton Department Stores, Inc. ("BTDS"); The Bon-Ton Giftco, LLC; Carson Pirie Scott II, Inc.; Bon- Ton Distribution, LLC; McRIL, LLC; Bonstores Holdings One, LLC; Bonstores Realty One, LLC; Bonstores Holdings Two, LLC; and Bonstores Realty Two, LLC. Case 18-10248- MFW Doc 632-1 Filed 04/18/18 Page 3 of 60 facilities) identified on Exhibit 1(a)(2) attached hereto (each a "Distribution Center" and collectively, the "Distribution Centers") by means of a "going out of business," "store closing," "sale on everything," "everything must go," or similar sale as described further below (the "GOB Sale"), with the nature and manner of advertising the GOB Sale being in Agent's sole discretion, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the Sale Guidelines and Approval Order (each as defined below);
perturbation.explanation: Changing "exclusive agent" to "non-exclusive agent" directly contradicts several clauses that grant the Agent exclusive rights and control over aspects of the sale, introducing uncertainty about the Agent's authority and Merchant's ability to engage other agents. The perturbation contradicts with the following line in the agreement 'Agent shall have the exclusive right to use the Stores and all other Assets for the purpose of conducting the GOB Sale, free of any interference from any entity or person, subject to compliance with the Sale Guidelines (as defined below) and Approval Order;'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Agent shall have the exclusive right to use the Stores and all other Assets for the purpose of conducting the GOB Sale, free of any interference from any entity or person, subject to compliance with the Sale Guidelines (as defined below) and Approval Order;
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Agent is the "exclusive agent", whereas the changed text states the Agent is the "non-exclusive agent". This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the "Agent shall have the exclusive right to use the Stores and all other Assets for the purpose of conducting the GOB Sale". A non-exclusive agent would not have the 'exclusive right' to conduct the sale, as the Merchant would be allowed to use other agents as well. This is a strong contradiction that would cause confusion and legal issues.

perturbation.original_text: Agent shall have the exclusive right to use the Stores and all other Assets for the purpose of conducting the GOB Sale, free of any interference from any entity or person, subject to compliance with the Sale Guidelines (as defined below) and Approval Order;
perturbation.changed_text: Agent shall have a non-exclusive right to use the Stores and all other Assets for the purpose of conducting the GOB Sale, subject to compliance with the Sale Guidelines (as defined below) and Approval Order;
perturbation.explanation: Changing "exclusive right" to "non-exclusive right" directly contradicts several clauses that grant the Agent exclusive rights and control over aspects of the sale, introducing uncertainty about the Agent's authority and Merchant's ability to engage other agents. The perturbation contradicts with the following line in the agreement 'WHEREAS, Merchant operates retail stores and desires that the Agent act as Merchant's exclusive agent for the purposes of:'. This means now there is no certainty who is making decisions for selling the assets.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, Merchant operates retail stores and desires that the Agent act as Merchant's exclusive agent for the purposes of: (a) selling all of the Merchandise (as hereinafter defined) from Merchant's retail store locations identified on Exhibit 1(a)(1) attached hereto (each a "Store" and collectively the "Stores") and distribution centers (including e-commerce 1 Merchant consists of The Bon-Ton Stores, Inc.; The Bon-Ton Department Stores, Inc. ("BTDS"); The Bon-Ton Giftco, LLC; Carson Pirie Scott II, Inc.; Bon- Ton Distribution, LLC; McRIL, LLC; Bonstores Holdings One, LLC; Bonstores Realty One, LLC; Bonstores Holdings Two, LLC; and Bonstores Realty Two, LLC. Case 18-10248- MFW Doc 632-1 Filed 04/18/18 Page 3 of 60 facilities) identified on Exhibit 1(a)(2) attached hereto (each a "Distribution Center" and collectively, the "Distribution Centers") by means of a "going out of business," "store closing," "sale on everything," "everything must go," or similar sale as described further below (the "GOB Sale"), with the nature and manner of advertising the GOB Sale being in Agent's sole discretion, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the Sale Guidelines and Approval Order (each as defined below);
perturbation.justification: The change from "exclusive right" to "non-exclusive right" directly contradicts the 'WHEREAS' clause, which explicitly states that the Agent will act as the Merchant's "exclusive agent." This introduces a clear and direct conflict regarding the Agent's authority and creates uncertainty about whether the Merchant can engage other agents. The changed text breaks the consistency and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: At any time prior to the earlier to occur of (i) the end of the Designation Rights Period applicable to a particular Lease or Contract and (ii) the Designation Rights Termination Date, Purchaser shall have the right, which right may be exercised at any time and from time to time, to file a Lease/Contract Assumption Notice in the Bankruptcy Cases designating the assignee (which may in certain circumstances be Purchaser, any of the entities comprising Purchaser, any of their respective affiliates, and/or a new entity created by any of the foregoing) of one or more Leases and/or Contracts (which may occur without further order of the Bankruptcy Court pursuant to the Approval Order) and setting forth the proposed cure amount due pursuant to section 365 of the Bankruptcy Code.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time prior to the earlier to occur of (i) the end of the Designation Rights Period applicable to a particular Lease or Contract and (ii) the Designation Rights Termination Date, Purchaser shall have the right, which right may NOT be exercised at any time and from time to time, to file a Lease/Contract Assumption Notice in the Bankruptcy Cases designating the assignee (which may in certain circumstances be Purchaser, any of the entities comprising Purchaser, any of their respective affiliates, and/or a new entity created by any of the foregoing) of one or more Leases and/or Contracts (which may occur without further order of the Bankruptcy Court pursuant to the Approval Order) and setting forth the proposed cure amount due pursuant to section 365 of the Bankruptcy Code.
perturbation.explanation: Introducing "NOT be exercised" into the clause regarding the Purchaser's right to file a Lease/Contract Assumption Notice creates a direct contradiction. The original text grants the Purchaser the power to file such notices at any time, but the modified text suggests that the Purchaser does not have this power, even though it is crucial for the assumption and assignment of leases and contracts. This directly undermines the purpose of the Lease/Contract Designation Rights and introduces uncertainty as to whether Purchaser has that power, despite the rest of the contract assuming it to be true. the Perturbation contradicts with the following line: 'Purchaser shall have the exclusive right to designate the assignees of Merchant's right, title, and interest in and to any or all of the Leases and Contracts (the "Lease/Contract Designation Rights")'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Purchaser shall have the exclusive right to designate the assignees of Merchant's right, title, and interest in and to any or all of the Leases and Contracts (the "Lease/Contract Designation Rights") upon the terms and conditions agreed upon between Purchaser and such designee.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text explicitly grants the Purchaser the right to file a Lease/Contract Assumption Notice "at any time and from time to time." The changed text negates this right by stating it may "NOT be exercised at any time and from time to time." This directly contradicts the clause in the contradicted text, which states "Purchaser shall have the exclusive right to designate the assignees of Merchant's right, title, and interest in and to any or all of the Leases and Contracts." If the Purchaser does NOT have the right to file the Lease/Contract Assumption Notice, their ability to designate assignees (Lease/Contract Designation Rights) is directly impeded, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction.
